chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 1173 of 2022 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge Fresh case 1. For orders on CMA No.4397/2022 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objections at Flag-A 3. For orders on CMA No.4398/2022 (Ex./A) 4. For hearing of main case 22-09-2022 Petitioner Muhammad Malook, present in person..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- 1. Urgency is granted. 3. Exemption is granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 2 & 4. The petitioner claiming to be a social activist has maintained instant petition, inter alia, seeking directions to respondents No.2 and 3 to enquire into the matter and take legal action against the persons involved in the corruption. It is stated by the petitioner that since 2020 he knows about the corruption committed by the respondents especially respondents No.5 to 10 and about the involvement of private persons, who are causing huge losses to SEPCO by committing theft of electricity. The petitioner has failed to bring on record any material to establish that at any particular premises and with the connivance of owner thereof, the respondents-officials of Sukkur Electric Power Supply Company (SEPCO) are committing theft of electricity. Even under the relevant law, the officials of SEPCO are authorized to take legal action by instituting criminal proceedings against the persons involved in commission of theft of electricity. The instant petition has been filed vaguely on the basis of presumption and assumption, which cannot be the base for grant of relief prayed by the petitioner; hence, this petition is dismissed in limine. J U D G E J U D G E Abdul Basit
